arguments.task: Open and read official SendGrid first-party pages needed to determine: one-line product/API description, documented API authentication methods, pricing or plan/access gating for API use, API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and whether an official MCP server/integration exists. Prefer canonical docs/pricing pages that fully load in browser. Return the final answer with exact canonical URLs read and short evidence notes tied to each URL. Also inspect SendGrid developer docs for authentication and API reference, pricing pages for plan tiers, and search the official docs/site for MCP.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official SendGrid pages and extract canonical evidence.

raw_result.description: Cloud email delivery platform with APIs for sending email and managing email infrastructure and engagement data.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official SendGrid v3 API authentication docs show API-key authentication using an Authorization header with a Bearer API key. No first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2, Basic, or a separate API-key query/header scheme beyond bearer token use.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official SendGrid pricing pages are referenced from first-party navigation and SendGrid publicly offers self-serve plans, including a free tier, rather than requiring partner approval or contact-sales access just to start using the API.

api_surface.breadth: Public SendGrid v3 API reference documents many REST endpoints for mail send, templates, suppressions, marketing contacts/campaigns, statistics, webhooks, sender identities, and account management.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Based on the evidence gathered, no official SendGrid MCP server or MCP integration documentation was found on first-party pages. This remains uncertain because MCP evidence was not conclusively verified from a dedicated official page.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs say limits are endpoint-specific and communicated via X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers, with 429 responses when exceeded.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs expose a broad API reference and mention official libraries/SDKs for multiple languages, which is a strong build signal for an agent toolkit.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Access-tier evidence was not verified from a successfully read canonical pricing page during this pass, and official MCP absence was not conclusively supported by a dedicated first-party page. The structured fields access_tier and mcp_exists therefore remain partially uncertain.
